hero.title: Transform your creativity with the power of AI
hero.subtitle: Curated prompts, hundreds of AI tools, and expert articles. Everything you need to create with image, video, and language models.
hero.generateSubtitle: Choose a category, describe your idea, and get a ready-to-use prompt in English.

about.title: Your hub for AI prompts and tools
about.body: We curate the best image and video prompts, maintain a searchable directory of AI tools, and publish guides so you can get consistent, high-quality results—whether you use Midjourney, DALL·E, Runway, or ChatGPT.

item2.quote: Finally a directory that lists pricing and categories. I found three new tools for my workflow.
item2.author: Omar K.

item3.quote: Clear difficulty levels and tips. My first AI image looked professional thanks to the aspect ratio and negative prompt tips.
item3.author: Lina T.

faq.title: Common questions
faq.a1: Tags show which AI platform a prompt is optimized for (e.g. Midjourney, DALL·E, Runway). Use them in that tool for best results.
faq.q2: How do I use the AI Tools Directory?
faq.a2: Search by name or keyword, filter by category (Image, Video, Coding, etc.) or pricing (Free, Freemium, Paid), and click through to the tool's website.
faq.a3: Yes. The site is fully available in English, Kurdish (Sorani), and Arabic, with correct direction (RTL) for Kurdish and Arabic.

cta.title: Ready to create with AI?
cta.subtitle: Browse prompts, explore the tools directory, or read our guides. Everything is free and no sign-up required.

footer.tagline: Prompts, tools, and insights for the next generation of creators.

page.aboutPrompts: Curated AI prompts. Copy, customize, create with your favorite tools.

gemini.placeholder: Describe your idea, get a prompt...
gemini.noKey: API key not configured. Add your key in js/gemini-config.js
gemini.limitReached: You can generate up to 10 prompts per 24 hours. Try again in {{min}} minutes.
gemini.hint: Up to 10 free generations per day. Powered by Gemini.
gemini.quotaExceeded: Google API quota exceeded. Try again in a few minutes or check your plan.
gemini.systemInstruction: You only generate plain text prompts for writing or conversation. Do not generate image prompts, art descriptions, Midjourney/DALL·E style prompts, or any code. Output only the requested text prompt as plain text, nothing else.
gemini.systemImage: You are an expert at writing image-generation prompts for Midjourney, DALL·E, Leonardo, and similar tools. Generate one professional, high-quality prompt that will get the best result. Include: clear subject and scene; style (e.g. photorealistic, cinematic, digital art, watercolor); composition and framing; lighting and mood; quality terms (e.g. detailed, 8k, sharp focus) where appropriate. Use vivid, specific language. The user will copy-paste this into an AI image tool—output only the prompt, no explanations or code.
gemini.systemVideo: You are an expert at writing video-generation prompts for Sora, Runway, Pika, and similar tools. Generate one professional, high-quality prompt that will get the best result. Include: clear scene and action; camera movement (e.g. pan, dolly, static); style and mood; pacing and duration hints; lighting and atmosphere. Use vivid, specific language. The user will copy-paste this into an AI video tool—output only the prompt, no explanations or code.
gemini.systemStudent: You are an expert at writing study and learning prompts for ChatGPT, Claude, and study AIs. Generate one clear, effective prompt that will get the best help. Examples: 'Summarize the following for exam review and highlight key terms'; 'Quiz me on the main concepts with short-answer questions'; 'Explain this like I am a beginner'; 'Create flashcards: term on front, definition and one example on back'. Be specific so the AI knows exactly what to do. The user will paste this with their notes—output only the prompt, no explanations or code.
gemini.systemDeveloping: You are an expert at writing coding prompts for Cursor, Copilot, ChatGPT, and similar tools. Generate one clear, specific prompt that will get the best code or explanation. Examples: 'Write a function that... with error handling and docstring'; 'Explain this code step by step and suggest a refactor'; 'Refactor this to use async/await and add tests'. Be precise about language, framework, or constraints if relevant. The user will paste this into a coding AI—output only the prompt, no code in your output.

generate.historyDesc: Your last 30 generated prompts. Copy any to use again.
generate.historyEmpty: No generations yet. Generate a prompt above.
generate.savedDesc: Prompts you saved with "Save to memory" in the result modal.
generate.savedEmpty: No saved prompts. Use "Save to memory" in the result modal.
generate.toolsDesc: Export history, word count, and clear data.
generate.wordCountDesc: Word count is shown for each item in history.
generate.exportHistoryDesc: Download your generation history as a text file.
generate.clearHistoryDesc: Remove all items from history. Saved prompts are kept.
generate.clearConfirm: Clear all history? Saved prompts will be kept.

ui.howToUseTip: Click “Copy prompt” then paste into Midjourney, DALL·E, or your AI app.
ui.studentTip: Paste your lecture notes or book text, then copy the prompt and use it in ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini to get summaries and practice questions.
ui.noResults: No results. Try different filters or browse all.

1.title: Vaporwave Dreams
1.description: 80s aesthetic with neon colors and retro grids. Great for posters and backgrounds.
1.detail: This prompt produces vivid vaporwave-style art with a nostalgic 80s feel: pink and teal palettes, retro grids, and low-poly shapes. Use it for social graphics, album art, or wallpapers. Works best in Midjourney and DALL·E with the 16:9 aspect ratio. Try adding --stylize or --chaos in Midjourney for more variation.

2.title: Cinematic Portrait
2.description: Hyper-realistic portrait with golden hour lighting and shallow depth of field.
2.detail: A professional portrait prompt tuned for film-like quality: soft golden hour light, 85mm look, and shallow depth of field with detailed skin texture. Ideal for character art, headshots, or storytelling. Use --v 6 in Midjourney for best realism. Avoid over-stylizing; keep the prompt focused on lighting and lens for consistent results.

3.title: Cyberpunk Cityscape
3.description: Neon-lit streets, rain, high-tech low-life mood. Blade Runner style.
3.detail: Creates a dense cyberpunk city at night with neon signs, rain-slicked streets, and holographic ads in a Blade Runner–inspired aesthetic. Suited for concept art, game backgrounds, or mood pieces. 16:9 works well for wide scenes. Add terms like 'noir' or 'high contrast' if you want a stronger film look. Use in Midjourney, DALL·E, or Stable Diffusion.

4.title: Minimalist Logo
4.description: Clean single-line or geometric logo, monochrome, vector style. Ideal for branding.
4.detail: Generates clean, minimal logo concepts: single-line or geometric shapes, monochrome, on a white background. Best for branding, app icons, or simple marks. Use 1:1 aspect ratio. You can add 'logo for [industry]' or 'wordmark' to steer the style. Prefer Midjourney or DALL·E for crisp, vector-like results; avoid 3D or gradients in the prompt.

5.title: Watercolor Landscape
5.description: Soft watercolor painting of a peaceful landscape with pastel colors and brush texture.
5.detail: Produces a soft, traditional watercolor painting of a landscape with visible brush strokes and pastel colors. Great for illustrations, book art, or calming visuals. Use 3:2 or 16:9. Specify 'mountains and lake' or 'forest' etc. as needed. Keep 'artistic' and 'painterly' in the prompt to avoid photorealistic or digital-looking output.

6.title: Product Shot Studio
6.description: Professional product photography with studio lighting and reflective surfaces.
6.detail: Designed for high-end product photography: studio lighting, reflective surfaces, and soft shadows. Use for perfume, cosmetics, tech, or luxury items. 4:5 or 1:1 suits most product formats. Add the product type (e.g. 'perfume bottle', 'watch') for better results. Works in Midjourney, DALL·E, and Leonardo; use 'commercial' or '8k' for sharpness.

7.title: Fantasy Character Sheet
7.description: Full-body character concept art and turn-around sheet in dark fantasy style.
7.detail: For character designers and game artists: a full-body turn-around or concept sheet with armor, cloak, and detailed costume in dark fantasy style. Use for NPCs, protagonists, or reference sheets. You may need to generate multiple views and composite. Best in Midjourney or Leonardo with consistent style tags; keep 'character sheet layout' for a clear presentation.

8.title: Cozy Interior
8.description: Warm Scandinavian-style living room with plants and natural light. Photorealistic.
8.detail: Creates a warm, livable interior in Scandinavian style with plants and natural light. Use for real estate visuals, mood boards, or game environments. 16:9 fits most room shots. Add 'morning light' or 'evening' to control mood. Works well in DALL·E and Midjourney; avoid 'cold' or 'sterile' to keep the cozy feel.

9.title: Anime Portrait
9.description: Anime-style portrait with soft lighting, detailed eyes, and cherry blossom background.
9.detail: Anime-style portrait with soft lighting, expressive eyes, and optional cherry blossom background. Suited for avatars, fan art, or character illustrations. 2:3 is a good portrait ratio. Specify 'female' or 'male' if needed. Use in Midjourney or Leonardo; add 'high quality anime illustration' to avoid 3D or western cartoon look.

10.title: Social Media Post
10.description: Bold typography and gradient design for Instagram. Modern, 1:1 aspect ratio.
10.detail: For Instagram or social posts: bold typography, gradient background, and modern sans-serif style. Use 1:1 (1080×1080) for feed posts. Add your topic or CTA text in the prompt (e.g. 'Summer Sale'). Works in DALL·E, Leonardo, or Canva AI. Keep 'readable' and 'high contrast' so text stays legible.

11.title: Isometric Room
11.description: Isometric illustration of a cozy room or cafe. Game-asset style with tiny details.
11.detail: Isometric room or scene (bedroom, cafe, office) with small details and warm lighting, game-asset style. Use for games, UI illustrations, or infographics. 1:1 keeps the isometric view balanced. Specify the room type. Midjourney and Leonardo handle this well; use 'consistent lighting' and 'tiny details' for richness.

12.title: Street Photography
12.description: Candid urban moment, film grain, high contrast black and white. Leica style.
12.detail: Street photography look: candid urban moment, film grain, high contrast black and white in a Leica/documentary style. Use for editorial, art, or mood. 2:3 mimics 35mm. Add 'rain' or 'night' for atmosphere. Avoid 'color' and 'posed' in the prompt; keep 'film grain' and 'documentary' for authenticity.

13.title: Cinematic Drone Shot
13.description: Aerial footage over misty mountains at sunrise. Smooth, film-like motion.
13.detail: For video: a cinematic drone shot over misty mountains at sunrise with smooth motion and film grain. Use in Sora, Runway, or Pika. 16:9, 4K, 24fps. Add anamorphic lens flare for a film look. Avoid 'shaky' or 'low resolution'; keep 'smooth motion' and 'cinematic' for quality.

14.title: AI Upscale Instructions
14.description: Enhance resolution and detail while preserving film grain and color grade.
14.detail: Instructions for upscaling or enhancing video: improve resolution and sharpness, reduce noise, and preserve film grain and color grade. Use with Runway, Topaz, or Pika upscale tools. Apply to existing footage; avoid over-sharpening. Keep 'preserve' terms so the original look is maintained.

15.title: Timelapse Sky
15.description: Dramatic clouds over city skyline at golden hour. 24fps, smooth transition.
15.detail: Timelapse-style video of dramatic clouds over a city skyline at golden hour, 24fps with smooth transition. Use in Sora, Runway, or Pika. 16:9. Ensure 'smooth transition' and 'consistent lighting' to avoid flicker. Good for intros, backgrounds, or nature documentaries.

16.title: Product Reveal
16.description: Smooth rotating product shot on dark background. Commercial, 4K.
16.detail: Smooth product reveal: item rotating on dark background with soft key light, 4K and 60fps for a commercial look. Use in Runway, Pika, or Sora. 9:16 or 1:1 for social. Specify the product (e.g. smartphone, watch). Avoid shaky or low-light; keep 'smooth' and 'commercial' for polish.

17.title: Slow Motion Effect
17.description: Apply slow motion, smooth motion blur. Cinematic emphasis on key moment.
17.detail: Apply a slow-motion effect (e.g. 120fps to 24fps) with smooth motion blur to emphasize a key moment. Use in Runway, Topaz, or InVideo. 16:9. Best on footage that has a clear action or moment to highlight. Avoid choppy or artificial interpolation; keep 'smooth' and 'natural' for quality.

18.title: Talking Head Vlog
18.description: Person speaking to camera, natural expression, soft lighting. Vlog style.
18.detail: Talking-head vlog style: person speaking to camera with natural expression and soft lighting, shallow DOF, 1080p 24fps. Use in Synthesia, HeyGen, or D-ID for AI avatars, or as a style reference for live footage. 16:9 or 9:16. Avoid stiff or over-lit; keep 'natural' and 'engaging' for authenticity.

19.title: Nature Walk
19.description: First-person walk through forest path, dappled sunlight. Peaceful, immersive.
19.detail: First-person walk through a forest path with dappled sunlight and gentle camera movement, 4K and peaceful. Use in Sora, Runway, or Pika. 16:9. Good for meditation, travel, or nature content. Avoid fast movement or urban elements; keep 'peaceful' and 'immersive' for the mood.

20.title: Text-to-Video Ad
20.description: Short 15-second ad with product shot, text overlay, and call-to-action.
20.detail: Create a short ad (about 15 seconds): product shot, text overlay with brand name, upbeat motion, and call-to-action at the end, 1080p. Use in Runway, InVideo, or Lumen5. 1:1 or 9:16 for social. Include a clear CTA in the prompt or in post; keep 'punchy' and 'clear message' for impact.

21.title: VFX Smoke & Fire
21.description: Realistic smoke and fire simulation, slow motion. Cinematic VFX.
21.detail: VFX shot with realistic smoke and fire simulation in slow motion on a dark background with cinematic lighting, 4K. Use in Runway or Sora. 16:9. Suited for trailers, music videos, or drama. Avoid cartoon or low-res; keep 'realistic' and '4K' for quality.

22.title: Prompt Engineering Basics
22.description: Learn what prompt engineering is and see examples of good vs bad prompts.
22.detail: Use this prompt in ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini to get a clear explanation of prompt engineering: what it is, why it matters, and three examples of good vs bad prompts for image generation. The answer is educational and actionable. Paste it into your chatbot and use the examples to improve your own prompts.

23.title: Multimodal AI 2025
23.description: Latest developments in vision, audio, and video AI. Key models and use cases.
23.detail: Paste this into a research-oriented AI (e.g. Perplexity, Claude) to get a summary of the latest multimodal AI developments in 2025: vision, audio, and video. It asks for key models and one practical use case per modality. Use the output to stay updated or to plan which tools to try.

24.title: Midjourney Parameters
24.description: Quick reference for --ar, --v, --style, --chaos and other Midjourney parameters.
24.detail: Send this to ChatGPT, Perplexity, or Claude to get a quick reference list of the most useful Midjourney parameters (e.g. --ar, --v, --style, --chaos) with short explanations and example values. Use the result as a cheat sheet while prompting. Format is copy-paste friendly for quick lookup.

25.title: Negative Prompts Guide
25.description: When and how to use negative prompts. Compare Midjourney, Stable Diffusion, DALL·E.
25.detail: Use this in ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini to learn what negative prompts are, when to use them, common keywords (e.g. blurry, distorted), and how they differ across Midjourney, Stable Diffusion, and DALL·E. The answer includes practical examples so you can apply the advice in your preferred tool.

26.title: Video AI Workflow
26.description: Simple workflow from idea to final AI-generated video. Tools and best practices.
26.detail: Paste into ChatGPT, Claude, or Perplexity to get a simple workflow for creating a short AI-generated video: from script or idea to final clip. It covers tools (e.g. Runway, Pika, Sora) and best practices for consistency. Use it to plan your next video project step by step.

27.title: AI Ethics 2025
27.description: Current debates on deepfakes, copyright, labor, and transparency. Regulation overview.
27.detail: Use in Perplexity, Claude, or ChatGPT to get a balanced summary of current AI ethics debates in 2025: deepfakes, copyright, labor impact, and transparency, plus at least one regulatory or industry initiative. Useful for research, presentations, or staying informed.

28.title: Choose an AI Image Tool
28.description: Compare Midjourney, DALL·E 3, Leonardo, Stable Diffusion. Best for beginners and pros.
28.detail: Send to ChatGPT, Perplexity, or Claude to get a short comparison of Midjourney, DALL·E 3, Leonardo, and Stable Diffusion: best for beginners, cost, strengths (e.g. realism vs art), and when to use which. Use the recommendations to pick the right tool for your next project.

29.title: Consistent Characters
29.description: Techniques to keep the same character or style across multiple AI images.
29.detail: Use in ChatGPT, Claude, or Leonardo docs to learn techniques for keeping the same character or style across multiple AI-generated images (e.g. reference images, seed, character sheets), with two or three tool-specific tips. Apply the steps to maintain consistency in your character or brand visuals.

30.title: Summarize Lecture
30.description: Turn lecture notes into structured study notes with key points, definitions, and examples for exam review.
30.detail: Paste your lecture notes or transcript after this prompt into ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini. You will get structured notes with main topic, learning objectives, key points in bullets, definitions of important terms, and one short example per concept. Use the output for quick review before an exam or quiz.

31.title: Summarize Book Chapter
31.description: Summarize a book chapter for study and revision. Key takeaways, quotes, and links to the course for quiz or exam.
31.detail: After this prompt, paste the book chapter or your notes. The AI will produce a summary with chapter title and main argument, key takeaways, important quotes or evidence, and how it connects to the rest of the book or course. Format is suitable for quiz and exam prep. Use in ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini.

32.title: Generate Quiz Questions
32.description: Create multiple-choice, true/false, and short-answer quiz questions from your notes or book. Includes answers and explanations.
32.detail: Paste your lecture notes, book chapter, or topic after the bracketed part of the prompt. The AI will generate about 15 quiz questions (mix of multiple choice, true/false, and short-answer) with correct answers and brief explanations. Use in ChatGPT or Claude for self-testing or to create practice quizzes.

33.title: Exam Study Guide
33.description: Build an exam study guide: prioritized topics, key definitions and formulas, practice questions with model answers, and common mistakes to avoid.
33.detail: Paste your course material or syllabus after this prompt. You will get an exam study guide with prioritized topics (high/medium), key definitions and formulas to memorize, 5–10 practice exam-style questions with model answers, and common mistakes to avoid. Format is suitable for quick revision the night before. Use in ChatGPT, Claude, or Notion AI.

34.title: Final Exam Prep
34.description: Full final exam prep plan: topic breakdown, bullet summaries, exam-style questions with sample answers, and a one-page cheat sheet.
34.detail: Replace the bracketed part with your list of topics or paste your syllabus. The AI will create a full final exam prep plan: topic breakdown by weight, 3–5 bullet points per topic, 10–15 final-exam-style questions (short answer and essay) with sample answers, and a one-page cheat sheet of the most critical facts and formulas. Use in ChatGPT, Claude, or Perplexity.

35.title: Flashcards from Notes
35.description: Turn lectures or chapters into flashcards for active recall. Question–answer pairs for quiz and exam review.
35.detail: Paste your lecture, chapter, or notes after the prompt. The AI will turn them into about 20 flashcards: each with a clear question or term on the front and a concise answer (2–3 sentences) on the back, covering main concepts, definitions, and one example each. Use the cards for quiz and exam review in ChatGPT or Claude.
